L1.area: Model deployment and serving container initialization logic

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where dependency specifications read from a model artifact's configuration file are directly interpolated into a shell command string without sanitization. An attacker who supplies a malicious model artifact can achieve arbitrary command execution on the system that deploys the model.

L3.area: Model serving container dependency installation and environment setup logic
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where dependency specifications read from a model artifact's Python environment YAML configuration are directly interpolated into a shell command string without sanitization when deploying with the local environment manager. An attacker who supplies a malicious model artifact can achieve arbitrary command execution on the system that deploys the model.

ground_truth.reason: Dependency strings from a model artifact's python_env.yaml are joined and interpolated directly into a bash -c shell command, allowing command injection through crafted dependency specifications.
